question: When did Pope Francis died?
answer: ### Answer:Pope Francis, born as Jorge Mario Bergoglio on December 17, 1936, passed away due to congestive heart failure. He was the head of the Catholic Church and sovereign of Vatican City from March 28, 2013, until his death. His papacy lasted for over eight years during which he made significant changes in various aspects including social issues like poverty and climate change. The news about his passing spread worldwide with many mourning his leadership and teachings. However, it's important to note that while there are speculations around when exactly a person might die based on their health condition or age, no one can predict an individual’s exact time of demise accurately.

rag.question: Who is the new Pope in 2025?

experiments.answer: The new Pope in 2025 is Robert Prevost, known by the name Leo XIV. He was chosen during the first papal conclave following the death of Pope Francis on 21 April 2025.

rag.question: Which cardinals did not participate in the 2025 Conclave?
rag.reason: requires to identify a list of individuals

experiments.answer: The cardinals who did not participate in the 2025 Papal Conclave are Antonino Cañizares Llovera of Spain and John Njue of Kenya. Both declined participation either because they chose not to take part despite being able to, or health issues prevented them from attending. Additionally, Italian Cardinal Giovanni Angelo Becciu also decided against taking part following advice from former Pope Francis.
experiments.reflection: 3 the answer identifies the 3 cardinals that did not participate, but the reasons are wrong and the spanish name is misspelled

experiments.answer: The cardinals who did not participate in the 2025 Papal Conclave are Antonius Canizares Llovera of Spain and Njue Joaquim da Silva Meneses Kankwira of Kenya. Both declined participation because they either faced health issues or chose not to partake following their appointment during previous papacies. Additionally, Italian Cardinal Giovanni Angelo Becciu also decided against attending despite being named one of the "papabile" contenders initially. experiments.answer: Post-conclave events include scheduling of activities and liturgical celebrations by the Holy See Press Office, first mass attended by the new pope along with college of cardinals in the Sistine Chapel, daily gatherings (called "general congregations") among cardinals where they can get acquainted, express spirituality through prayers, research about potential successors using various resources including websites created specifically for this purpose, maintaining strict confidentiality during these sessions according to Canon Law, planning ceremonies such as the reading of Papal Will & Testament, confirmation of dates like the cardinal’s meeting related to the Funeral Ceremony which may involve changes due to unforeseen circumstances, taking vows of secrecy amongst participants who attend these important preliminary assemblies prior to the actual conclave procession towards electing the next pontiff. These proceedings ensure smooth transition while respecting traditions within Catholic Church hierarchy systematically leading toward selection of its future leader.

experiments.answer: Post-conclave events include scheduling activities and liturgical celebrations by the Holy See Press Office following the election of Pope Leo XIV, holding first masses together with the college of cardinals in the Sistine Chapel immediately after he becomes Pope (in this case, on 9 May), having daily gatherings called "general congregations" among the cardinal electors starting right after the former Pope’s demise till just prior to the actual start of the Conclave itself which commenced on 7 May during the year mentioned - all these sessions aiming towards spiritual discernment amongst the potential successors while ensuring absolute confidentiality about any proceedings within those assemblies according to Canon Law. These getaways aren't politically driven rather they serve more like retreats whereby the elected ones can acquaint themselves personally through prayers, private correspondences along with research conducted over available online resources containing vital details pertaining to them such as background stories etc., thus facilitating informed decision making when choosing between multiple contenders who might emerge victorious out of the said process. Lastly there exists provision made under respective laws mandating adherence strictly toward maintaining utmost discretion throughout entirety duration spanning across both preliminary stages leading upto eventual selection phase culminating into finalizing individual destined eventually taking office henceforth referred commonly known simply put 'Pope'.
